highlights.text: I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.

I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “Another option to long is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at 6693.” Early Wednesday morning we flushed 6693 down to 6680, recovered 6693, then went parabolic to 6800 high last week.
Bearish calls were appearing, which meant it was time for institutions to buy the dip, and us to follow.

Ultimately though, ES found itself rangebound (mostly 6800 to 6750 with 6765 being a big pivot in the middle) sice late last week as it digests last Wednesday’s parabolic run, and prepares for the next move.
ES is rangebound in a big range 6750 to 6800, with 6765 being a big magnet/pivot in the middle.

post_title: After 7 Green Days, SPX Cools Off Today. Will The Mini-Dip Get Bought? Oct 8th Plan
